nodes.label: The Internal Combustion Engine
nodes.summary: The heart of the vehicle; its core architecture, cylinder configuration, and displacement directly dictate the car's power, efficiency, and overall performance characteristics.

nodes.label: Essential Supporting Systems
nodes.summary: An engine cannot survive alone. Vital sub-systems—like cooling, lubrication, and electrical—work continuously behind the scenes to prevent catastrophic failure and keep the vehicle running smoothly.

nodes.label: Fuel Dynamics & Selection
nodes.summary: Engines are precision-engineered for specific fuel types (gasoline, diesel, or hybrid blends). Introducing the wrong fuel type can lead to severe, irreversible engine damage.

nodes.label: The Transmission System
nodes.summary: The critical bridge between raw engine power and the wheels. It manages gear ratios to ensure the engine operates within its optimal RPM range across varying speeds.

nodes.label: Maintenance & Longevity
nodes.summary: Routine care—such as timely oil changes, filter replacements, and belt inspections—is the single most effective way to maximize a vehicle's lifespan and retain its resale value.
